para_trans: Main article: "2045 Other vendors on goods and services
Taxes and Duties "Year 2025-26 Final of this financial year
With regard to the provision and the return of the amount of savings (mandatory)
Government of Maharashtra
Industries, Energy, Labour and Mining Department,
Government Decision No. FMG-2026 / Q. No.36 / Energy-3
Mantralaya, Mumbai - 400032.
Date: March 31, 2026
The Government's Decision-
Demand No. K-1, Main Account Heading 2045 Other Taxes on Vendor Goods and Services in this Financial Year 2025-26
The total provision made under Fee (Compulsory) (Datum) is Rs. 98,90,49, 000 / - (Rupees Ninety Eight Crore Ninety Lakhs)
Ninety-nine thousand only) Rs. 66,79,54, 766 / - (Rupees Sixty-six crore eighty-nine lakh fifty-four thousand seven hundred and sixty-six) Final
Grants / Provisions are being made and out of the provision, Rs. 32,10,94, 234 / - (Rupees thirty-two crore ten lakh ninety-four thousand two hundred)
Thirty-four only) is being approved by the government for extradition and deposit in the title of reserve (Datta Mat).
The subheading-wise division of savings and the reasons are as follows.
(Rs. in thousands)
table_trans: Actual expenditure at the end of March 2026 (final provision)

Demand No.K-1, Main Account Header "2045 Other Taxes and Duties on Sales Goods and Services" 103, Recovery Expenditure - Electricity Charges Chief Electricity Inspector (00) (01) Electricity Charges Branch (Compulsory) 20450369

Out of the total 82 sanctioned posts, 44 vacancies resulted in the revised provision of Rs. 56213.00 thousand. As per the revised provision, funds of Rs. 36733.185 thousand were disbursed and the remaining provision was returned.
Actual expenditure at the end of March 2026 (final provision)

Due to non-submission of payments by the Chief Electrical Inspectorate, the revised provision has been increased to Rs. 16.00 thousand. Out of the revised provision, Rs. 252,000 has been disbursed and the remaining provision has been returned.
The entire provision has been surrendered as the contractual service was not provided by the Chief Electrical Inspectorate.

Under this account, out of the revised provision of Rs. 516.00 thousand, funds of Rs. 146.065 thousand have been disbursed and the remaining provision has been transferred. This saving is reflected in the fact that 44 out of the total 82 sanctioned posts in the Chief Electrical Inspectorate are vacant.

The revised provision under this account was made to Rs. 794.00 thousand. The savings are reflected in the non-submission of payments by the Chief Electricity Inspector's Office by disbursing funds as per the revised provision.
Actual expenditure at the end of March 2026 (final provision)

The revised provision under this ledger was raised to Rs. 2000.00 thousand. This saving is reflected in the receipt of a proposal from the Chief Electrical Inspectorate for a fund demand of Rs. 175.00 thousand.

Demand No.K-1, Main Account Header "2045 Other Taxes and Duties on Vendor Goods and Services" 103, Recovery Expenditure - Electricity Charges Office of the Chief Engineer (Electrical) (00) (00) (02) Electrical Inspection Branch (Mandatory) 20450378

Out of total 82 sanctioned posts in the office of the Chief Electrical Inspector, 44 posts are lying vacant and the revised provision has been made to the tune of Rs. 739815.00 thousand. Out of the revised provision, funds amounting to Rs. 607423.769 thousand have been disbursed and the remaining provision has been returned.
Due to reduced power consumption, the revised allocation was increased to Rs. 2684.00 thousand.
Actual expenditure at the end of March 2026 (final provision)
Out of the revised provision, a fund of Rs. 1505.84 thousand has been disbursed and the remaining provision has been returned.
As the contractual service was not provided, the symptomatic provision has been returned.

The revised provision under this account was made to Rs. 4154.00 thousand. Out of this provision, Rs. 2049.636 thousand has been disbursed and the remaining provision has been returned as the posts in the office of the Chief Electrical Inspector are vacant.

The revised provision under this ledger was raised to Rs. 9037.00 thousand. With the receipt of a proposal from the Chief Electrical Inspectorate for a fund demand of Rs. 6967.969 thousand, the remaining provision has been returned.
As per the demand received from the office of the Chief Electrical Inspector, the total revised allocation was increased to Rs. 13656.00 thousand.
Actual expenditure at the end of March 2026 (final provision)
A provision of Rs. 8010.673 thousand has been returned as the office has not spent the funds.

The Chief Electrical Inspector's Office had submitted a proposal for disbursement of funds. As such, the Department of Finance disbursed funds for payment of pending dues. However, the revised provision from the Chief Electrical Inspector's Office was Rs. 3160.00 thousand out of which Rs. 3036.953 thousand has been spent and the remaining provision has been returned.
As government vehicles were not available in this section, the revised provision was made to Rs. 80.00 thousand. The entire provision was returned.

The revised provision under this ledger was made to Rs. 32.00 thousand. This provision was made after the Chief Electricity Inspector's office did not receive a demand for funds for the advertisement.

It is 202605211610023910. The order is being digitally signed.
By order and in the name of the Governor of Maharashtra.
(No Mr. Karad)
Joint Secretary, Government of Maharashtra
